summary: YAML config loader with defaults, backward-compat migration from old openrouter section to new llm section, ~ path expansion, and eager validation

requires: Readable YAML file at the given path
guarantees: Returns nil and error on any missing required field
All path fields (session_file, db_path) have ~ expanded before return
LLM defaults applied: ollama URL defaults to localhost:11434, timeouts provider-specific
Refresh interval floored at 30 minutes if zero or negative

observations.text: Backward-compat migration: if cfg.LLM.Provider is empty but cfg.OpenRouter.APIKey is non-empty, the old openrouter section is promoted into cfg.LLM — allows old configs to work unchanged
validate() is called before expandPath on session_file and db_path — validation checks presence but not path existence, so ordering has no practical effect
OpenRouterConfig struct is retained as a migration source only; it is never used downstream after Load() applies the migration to LLMConfig
hackernews source requires feed to be non-empty at load time (validated), but reddit sort is not validated here — sort defaulting to 'hot' happens in main.go at registry construction time
LLM validation is gated on cfg.LLM.Provider != '' — an unconfigured LLM section is valid; the system runs without summarization capability
